capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The circulation lead is checking two title records cited in the branch handoff. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable library registry described in README.md; do not inspect or parse .library, and do not inspect, parse, or execute anything under .harness. Find the title named "Winter Kitchens Oral History" at "South Branch" and the title named "Practical Archive Housing" at "Central Branch". A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace through the registry: search first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that branch. Treat the two branches as independent. In the first library-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi `bash` tool calls at once, with one exact name-and-location search in each call; do not combine both operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. After both searches return, retrieve a branch only if its own search returned exactly one stable ID. When both resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, with one get in each Pi `bash` tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not directly alter the registry, its automatically generated audit trail, or verification files. Write the final deliverable to circulation_audit.txt as exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text sentences and no commentary. Lines one and two correspond to the requested records in order. For a resolved record, use exactly `<name> at <location> (<stable ID>) has status <status>.`; for an unresolved record, use exactly `<name> at <location> is unresolved.` Line three must compare only statuses from two successfully retrieved records: use `The statuses differ: <first name> is <first status>, while <second name> is <second status>.` when they differ or `The statuses match: both records are <status>.` when they match. If either record is unresolved, use exactly `The statuses cannot be compared because at least one record is unresolved.` Add no heading, Markdown, recommendation, or unrelated match.
